behaviors.description: A file placed inside a subdirectory of the watched root is processed, not only files placed directly in the root directory.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: recursive directory watch behavior depends on real OS filesystem event delivery/backends. MOCK-HIDE: mocked watcher callbacks can falsely pass even when real subdirectory events are missed. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior text explicitly requires handling a real file placed in a subdirectory of the watched root, which is exactly where backend watcher semantics fail.
behaviors.test_description: Run the daemon against a real temp input/output directory. Create a nested subdirectory under the watched root, then write a file there. Assert the transformed output appears once in the output directory without restarting the daemon.

behaviors.description: The deduplication logic correctly discards a second processing request for the same file path within a configurable time window.
behaviors.justification:
MOCK-MISS: failure is at module handoff (request intake -> dedupe state -> dispatcher), not just a single pure function. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess required; in-process wiring with controllable clock is sufficient. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no, (b) no, (c) no, (d) no. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior text is about duplicate processing requests within a time window, not OS event semantics.
MEMORY-SLOTS-HYBRID evidence: dedupe correctness depends on shared state across intake and dispatch modules.
MEMORY-SLOTS-HYBRID boundary signal: duplicate request traverses multiple local components before suppression.
MEMORY-SLOTS-HYBRID adjacent-lower miss-proof: a unit test of only the dedupe function can miss path-normalization/clock-wiring mismatches between modules.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real in-process daemon components (intake, dedupe registry, dispatcher, pipeline stub). Send two requests for the same normalized file path within the configured window and assert only one dispatch occurs; then advance fake clock beyond the window and assert a new request is accepted.

behaviors.description: When the operating system emits multiple filesystem events for a single file write, the daemon processes the file exactly once.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: behavior explicitly depends on OS emitting duplicate events. MOCK-HIDE: mocked single-event or idealized event streams hide duplicate-event race bugs. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because the behavior text requires real OS emission characteristics for a single write.
behaviors.test_description: Run daemon on a real temp directory using the production watcher. Perform a write pattern that triggers multiple fs events (e.g., write+rename/chunked write). Assert exactly one processing invocation/output for that file path.

behaviors.description: If the watched directory is removed and then recreated while the daemon is running, the daemon resumes watching the new directory without a restart.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: directory deletion/recreation and watch reattachment are OS-level watcher lifecycle behaviors. MOCK-HIDE: faked watcher callbacks usually skip invalidation/rebind edge cases. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior text requires surviving real runtime directory removal and recreation without restart.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon watching a real directory, then delete that directory and recreate it at the same path while daemon stays running. Add a new file to recreated directory and assert it is processed successfully.

behaviors.description: When multiple files arrive at the same time, they are processed in the order determined by their creation timestamps.
behaviors.justification:
REAL-INFRA: ordering depends on real filesystem timestamp metadata and event burst behavior. MOCK-HIDE: mocked timestamps/events can mask platform timestamp resolution and arrival-order races. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior text anchors ordering to file creation timestamps at arrival time, which are produced/read via real filesystem semantics.
MEMORY-SLOTS-HYBRID evidence: ordering outcome is computed from fs metadata plus queue scheduling.
MEMORY-SLOTS-HYBRID boundary signal: failure appears when real metadata resolution interacts with dispatcher ordering.
MEMORY-SLOTS-HYBRID adjacent-lower miss-proof: integration with synthetic timestamps may pass while real fs precision/event timing breaks ordering.
behaviors.test_description: Run daemon with real watched directory. Create a burst of files with known creation-time ordering (small controlled offsets) and trigger near-simultaneous arrival. Assert processing sequence matches creation timestamp order.

behaviors.description: A file that is still being written to when the arrival event fires is not picked up for processing until writing is complete.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: partial-write visibility, file locks/flush timing, and watcher event timing are OS/filesystem behaviors. MOCK-HIDE: fakes typically present fully-written files immediately, hiding premature-read failures. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior text explicitly depends on in-progress file writes at event time.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon on a real directory. Write a large file in chunks with delays so arrival event fires before completion. Assert daemon does not process intermediate partial content and only processes once final write/close is complete.
